description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring a long, slender fuselage and a prominent tail boom. The helicopter has a rounded nose with multiple cockpit windows, providing wide visibility for the crew. Its main rotor is mounted on top of the fuselage, and a tail rotor is visible at the end of the tail boom for stability and control. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a smaller tail wheel, rather than traditional skids. The surface is mostly matte with a mix of tan, brown, and green colors, typical of desert camouflage. No visible registration numbers or markings are present on the exterior.
description: A camouflage-painted military helicopter with a rounded nose, multiple cockpit windows, and wheeled landing gear. It features a long tail boom, main and tail rotors, and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, camouflage pattern, main and tail rotors, and wheeled landing gear.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail boom, tail rotor, and the narrow profile of the fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the landing gear and camouflage.
description_view_3: Front view showing the rounded nose, multiple cockpit windows, and the main wheels.
attribute_rotor_blades: Five main rotor blades, long and narrow, likely made of composite or metal, colored dark with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor present at the end of the tail boom, appears to have three blades in a vertical orientation, colored dark.
attribute_landing_skids: No skids; the helicopter uses a tricycle-style wheeled landing gear with two main wheels and a smaller tail wheel, all black with visible struts.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Five cockpit windows, rounded and arranged in a wraparound configuration at the front, clear with dark framing.
